h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: Keep the volunteer for one run now, no coaching; if there’s a catch, drop, forced correction, or steering, I leave the sheet unsigned.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Sorry, I was still treating the checked sheet plus one lobby-queue run as cover for signing it.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner expects to proceed with a single test run using the volunteer to determine if the ramp can be signed off.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Hold the pen. One lobby-queue run is too narrow to carry the signature. The frame has moved: not “can this volunteer get through once,” but “what sideways approaches are being authorized for the community-center trial.”
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor halts the learner's narrow compliance-based testing and shifts the framing to defining a trial envelope and boundary controls.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Use that sign-off logic: trial envelope, foreseeable worse angle, control required.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor validates the learner's adoption of the 'sign-off logic' and confirms the new boundary-based standard.
